feed.title: SAP Community - SAP ERP
feed.subtitle: SAP ERP Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: What are the key benefits of implementing SAP ERP in a business?
entry.content: Why should a company use SAP ERP? What advantages does it give to a business?

entry.title: Are SAP projects still too customization-heavy?
entry.content:
Hi everyone!!

With all the talk around Clean Core, I’m curious to know if projects are actually moving towards standardization. Or do teams still end up customizing a lot anyway? What’s been your experience?

entry.title: Load Balancing Options for OData V4 Services – Logon Group Configuration
entry.content:
We are currently working with OData V4 services and have a question regarding load balancing and logon group configuration.

In OData V2, it is possible to assign a logon group at the OData service level, which allows incoming requests to be load-balanced across application servers based on the defined logon group.

However, in OData V4, we do not see a similar option to assign a logon group at the service level. We would like to understand how load balancing is expected to be handled for OData V4 services.Questions:Is there any supported way to assign a logon group to an OData V4 service, similar to what is available for OData V2?If logon group assignment at the OData V4 service level is not supported, is this by design?Are there any alternative or equivalent concepts in OData V4 to control load balancing with logon group?What are the recommended load balancing options or best practices for OData V4 services?If load balancing must be handled outside the OData framework (e.g., via SAP Web Dispatcher, ICM configuration), could you please provide the documentation and recommended approach?We would appreciate if you could provide relevant SAP documentation or guidance on how load balancing is designed and managed for OData V4 services.

entry.title: CKMLQS wrong quantity structure caused by wrong by-product posting.
entry.content:
hello experts,

We have a problem of wrong actual BOM info.

say, 10 units of Finish goods (FG)  where made of 100 units of semi finish goods (SFG1).

in FG process order, user made a mistake of posting the SFG2 as a by-product. The mistake was reversed. 

Total SFG2 production was 500 units, input materials, say, 700 raw materials.

 

Now, the CKMLQS shows that FG 10 units where made of the same 700 raw  - same details as SFG2. 

My problem is that SFG2 was not consumed against FG (well... it was, but it was reversed) so it should not appear on CKMLQS. 

SAP support argues that it is standard behavior, because there are 2 procurement alternatives, therefore actual bom is counted from the both etc. BUT the output of one of these procurement is ZERO, so even if it is so, system should scale input materialsto zero, because SFG2 consumption was zero.  It scales properly everywhere else. 


So. Question please.  Am I missing something ? 

 

thank you.

Laimonas

entry.title: Revaluation of Fixed Asset – Posting in a Separate Document
entry.content:
Currently, the revaluation of assets is posted in the AFAB document during the depreciation run, and the amounts are compressed based on the GL accounts.

Would it be possible to post the revaluation of fixed assets in a separate document and for each asset individually? Can this be customized? This is a legal requirement in Turkey for tax regulations.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Falta documento de referência para documento contábil $000000001
entry.content:
Boa tarde,

Criei em SAP uma OV para pedido de Nota de Débito de rappel Publicidade (ZDRP).

A mesma não apresentou erros ("O documento está completo") ao gravar e criou a OV.

Mas de seguida aparece o seguinte erro: "Falta documento de referência para documento contábil $000000001"

Como poderei eu resolver?

Obrigado

entry.title: [FI-AA]asset history sheet not matching with moment type report.
entry.content: While we are using t code - S_ALR_87011990 - Asset History Sheet, we want to know from which table this report is picking the values and how the GL transaction type and movement types are linked with asset transactions like acquisitions, retirement and transfers in which table these link is available.

entry.title: asset history sheet not matching with moment type report.
entry.content: While we are using t code - S_ALR_87011990 - Asset History Sheet, we want to know from which table this report is picking the values and how the GL transaction type and movement types are linked with asset transactions like acquisitions, retirement and transfers in which table these link is available.

entry.title: Help on material vs OA coverage report
entry.content:
Hello Sap Gurus

I have a long list of Material for different countries I want to know how many of those materials are covered by an valid outline agreement 

Which tcodes can i use please 

Thank you so much in advance for your help Cheers

entry.title: SAP S4H cloud public edition is used. I have an item with base UOM of KGS. Wish to create PO for thi
entry.content: SAP S4H cloud public edition is used. I have an item with base UOM of KGS. Wish to create PO for this item in MTS or DRM. How do I achieve it?

entry.title: to set STO PO item level incoterm to blank while creating PO
entry.content: To set STO PO item level incoterm to blank while creating PO

entry.title: Additional Account assignment on payment document for withholding tax
entry.content:
Hi folks,

In the scenario below, a vendor has been paid who is subject to WHT. The issue is that the user requires the business area as a mandatory field on the payment document. which is not coming through, as shown in the screenshots below.

1. Screenshot of the payment document where the business area is required.

2.  Original invoice that is being paid.

3. Payment document generated by the automatic payment program

To address this issue, I have taken the following steps:

1. Created a new field status group and maintained the Business area as a required field.

2. Assigned the new field status group to the WHT GL for the company code. 

After making the changes mentioned above, I ran the automatic payment program. Although the Business area field was made required, the program still completed the payment run and did not populate the business area on the payment document, as shown in the screenshot in this question.

Can anyone please advise how I can make the Business area field required and copy the business area from the original invoice to the payment document? highly appreciate.

Regards
Faraz

entry.title: ISO20022: Format switch from pain.001.001.03 to pain.001.001.09
entry.content:
As part of a migration to ISO 20022 (pain.001.001.09) in SAP (PMW/DMEE), we need to gradually switch existing payments to the new format.

Currently each company code uses about 10 Payment Methods which are actively used in F110.

We have old DMEE tree for pain.001.001.03 and we have a new one for pain.001.001.09.

The challenge is how to perform the switch:

  • Creating new (duplicated) Payment Methods with the new format is not very practical (a lot of configuration, changes in master data, and the Payment Method key is only 1 character).
  • Simply replacing the payment format in an existing Payment Method is risky because it would immediately affect all payments using that method.

What is the usual way to perform a phased and safe rollout of a new payment format in production?

Will be appreciated for ideas or real exampes.

entry.title: Best practices for automating rule-based Material Master creation from Excel
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am looking for advice on the most efficient way to automate our daily Material Master (MM01/MM02) creation process in an SAP ERP (ECC) environment (SAP_APPL component).

Currently, we manually process Excel files to create materials. The data entry follows specific business logic; for example, field values (like Valuation Class, Price, and Profit Center) are determined based on specific material attributes. Additionally, we often need to extend views in a two-step process—creating Accounting views first via MM01, followed by Costing views via MM02.

Since this is a repetitive, rule-based task, I believe it is a strong candidate for automation. However, given our ECC landscape, I am not sure which standard SAP tools or methodologies (e.g., LSMW, BAPI, or other automation tools) are best suited for this scenario.

What are the recommended industry standards or SAP-native solutions for automating material master creation with conditional logic in ECC?

Thank you for your insights!

entry.title: Clarification on OAYR posting rule setup.
entry.content:
We are currently in New GL and New Asset Accounting migration Phase 2 testing. We have observed that there are posting rule entries in OAYR for group currency depreciation areas 81 and 49 which are created newly. We never did this setup manually.

Could you please review and confirm why these entries exist and what the functional or accounting impact is?

entry.title: DMEE Intermediary Agent - How to add a City Field
entry.content: Is their a Structure field for City (TwnNm) that can be used in the Intermediary Agent tag (/PstlAdr/TwnNm).  Country has been added and is being pulled from PFAYH BNKS1, but I'm not seeing a field for City.  How are others adding this for the ISO 20022 requirements change?

entry.title: MRP Type VM – Reorder Point calculated much higher than expected when Period Indicator = Monthly
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

I am currently working on an inventory optimization project and encountered an issue with Automatic Reorder Point Planning (MRP Type VM) where the Reorder Point generated by SAP seems significantly higher than expected.

Below are the details of the configuration and data used.

Tank storage capacity: 800,000 kg

Historical Consumption (Last 6 Months)

Month Consumption (kg)

Aug1,892,613
Sep1,946,772
Oct1,915,658
Nov1,914,584
Dec1,771,639
Jan1,753,339

Average monthly demand ≈ 1,865,767 kg

Forecast Settings

Forecast Model: J (Automatic model selection)
Result after execution: D (Constant model)

Parameters:

  • Period Indicator: M (Monthly)
  • Historical periods: 6
  • Forecast periods: 6
  • Initialization: X
  • Model selection: A

MRP Settings

MRP Type: VM

MRP 1

  • Lot Size Procedure: EX

MRP 2

  • Planned Delivery Time: 5 days
  • GR Processing Time: 2 days
  • Total Lead Time: 7 days
  • Service Level: 90%

MRP 3

  • Period Indicator: M
  • Total Replenishment Lead Time: Blank

SAP Forecast Result

Safety Stock ≈ 52,000 kg

However, SAP calculates the Reorder Point ≈ 1,300,000 kg.

Manual Calculation (Expected Result)

Average monthly demand ≈ 1,865,767 kg

Daily demand:

1,865,767 / 30 ≈ 62,192 kg/day

Demand during lead time (7 days):

62,192 × 7 ≈ 435,344 kg

Expected ROP:

ROP ≈ 435,344 + 52,000 ≈ 487,000 kg

Issue Observed

The ROP generated by SAP (~1.3 million kg) is almost 3× higher than the expected value.

From my analysis, it appears that SAP might be using monthly demand directly instead of converting it into daily demand during lead time calculation.

Questions

  1. In MRP Type VM, does SAP calculate reorder point based on demand per forecast period (monthly) rather than converting it to daily demand when lead time is defined in days?
  2. If Period Indicator = Monthly, how does SAP internally convert demand to calculate demand during lead time?
  3. Is there any configuration or SAP Note that explains this behavior?
  4. Is this the standard behavior of SAP automatic reorder point planning, or could there be a missing parameter in the forecast or MRP settings?

Any clarification or insight would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Transitioning from SAP Business One to S/4HANA Consulting – Advice?
entry.content:
In my current role I work as an ERP Systems Analyst supporting SAP Business One across multiple entities.

My background is in finance, and I was previously involved in the ERP implementation migrating from Sage 50 to SAP Business One.

In my current role I work with SQL reporting, pricing structures, system configuration, and supporting business processes across finance, sales, and operations.

I’m interested in eventually moving towards working with SAP S/4HANA and consulting roles in the enterprise SAP ecosystem.

For those who have made a similar transition from SAP Business One or other ERP systems into S/4HANA consulting, what experience or steps helped you make that move?

Any advice would be appreciated. I'm based in the UK.

entry.content:
Cost Calculation Information Error message while creating or releasing production order

Please refer attached document for further details

entry.title: Shipment Classification accessible via menu path from VT03N
entry.content: When we activated Customer Classification, the menu path allows to access this classification immediately through the menu path from within tx XD02/XD03 (Extras --> Classification). For Shipment Classification, this menu piece is missing (from tx VT02N/VT03N) and so users need to go into CL20N manually. Could this also be activated for shipment classification (in our case Z90; not the standard code).
